description: Fix defects found during comprehensive audit of the MCP server + Chrome extension background + side panel UI architecture. Eliminates race conditions, improves type safety, adds missing test coverage, and ensures deterministic behavior across the full three-layer stack.

userStories.title: Remove fragile sync.full delay in side panel ws:message fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the side panel to refresh its plugin list via event-driven notifications only, so that there are no magic timeout delays that can cause race conditions or stale UI.
acceptanceCriteria: The ws:message handler in App.tsx no longer uses setTimeout for sync.full

The ws:message handler for sync.full either: (a) is removed entirely because the sp:serverMessage plugins.changed path is sufficient, or (b) triggers loadPlugins when it receives a plugins.changed notification via the ws:message path
The primary sp:serverMessage path for plugins.changed continues to work unchanged
No magic number constants (1_500, 1500, etc.) remain in App.tsx related to sync.full delays
Side panel still refreshes correctly on initial connection (verified by reading the code flow)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx lines 125-137.

The ws:message fallback currently does:
```
if (wsData?.method === 'sync.full') {
  setTimeout(() => loadPluginsRef.current(), 1_500);
}
```

This 1.5s delay is a workaround for a race condition where the background hasn't finished processing sync.full yet. But the primary path already handles this correctly: handleSyncFull in message-router.ts sends a `plugins.changed` notification to the side panel AFTER all storage writes and injection complete (line 289-292 of message-router.ts). The side panel already handles `plugins.changed` in the sp:serverMessage handler (line 109-113 of App.tsx).

The safest fix: in the ws:message handler, instead of special-casing sync.full with a delay, handle `plugins.changed` the same way the sp:serverMessage path does (call loadPluginsRef.current()). The sync.full case can be removed since the background always sends plugins.changed after processing sync.full.

Alternatively, if you want to be extra defensive, you can keep listening for sync.full via ws:message but handle it the same way as plugins.changed in the sp:serverMessage path — just call loadPluginsRef.current() without a delay, since by the time the ws:message broadcast reaches the side panel, the background has already finished processing (the offscreen document broadcasts ws:message to all contexts simultaneously, and the background processes it synchronously before the side panel's listener fires in practice).

The simplest and most correct approach: change the sync.full case to also call `loadPluginsRef.current()` directly (no delay), matching how plugins.changed is handled. Or better yet, just handle plugins.changed in the ws:message path too.

userStories.title: Add E2E test coverage for side panel plugin list and tool toggle flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E test coverage for the side panel's core user flows (plugin list rendering, tool toggle), so that the full background-to-side-panel communication path is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: A new E2E test file or test block covers the side panel flow
Test verifies: side panel shows plugin list after MCP server connects
Test verifies: plugin card displays correct name, version, and tab state
Test verifies: tool toggle sends config.setToolEnabled to the MCP server
Test verifies: MCP server receives the tool config change and updates state
Tests use the existing Playwright fixtures from e2e/fixtures.ts
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Files: e2e/ directory. The existing fixtures in e2e/fixtures.ts already set up the MCP server, Chrome extension, and a test web server.

The challenge: Playwright needs to interact with the side panel. Chrome's side panel can be opened via chrome.sidePanel.open() or by clicking the extension icon. The existing E2E test setup loads the extension as an unpacked extension.

Approach:
1. Read the existing E2E tests (e2e/full-e2e.e2e.ts and e2e/lifecycle.e2e.ts) to understand the test patterns
2. The side panel is an extension page at chrome-extension://<id>/side-panel/side-panel.html — Playwright can navigate to it directly in a new page/tab
3. Open the side panel page directly via its URL (get extension ID from the loaded extension)
4. Wait for the plugin list to render (the side panel will connect to the background, which connects to the MCP server)
5. Verify plugin names, versions, and states appear
6. Click a tool toggle and verify the MCP server state changes

Key gotcha: The side panel page opened directly (not via chrome.sidePanel.open) behaves like a regular extension page. The comment in App.tsx lines 121-136 explains that ws:message fallback exists for exactly this scenario. This actually makes it easier to test.

Look at how e2e/fixtures.ts gets the extension ID — it likely uses chrome.management.getAll() or inspects the loaded extension path.

userStories.title: Add runtime version negotiation between side panel and MCP server
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the side panel to detect when it's running against an incompatible MCP server version, so that version mismatches are surfaced to the user instead of causing silent failures.
acceptanceCriteria: The config.getState response from the MCP server includes a protocolVersion field
A PROTOCOL_VERSION constant is defined in @opentabs-dev/shared and used by both MCP server and side panel
The side panel checks protocolVersion in the config.getState response on connect
If protocolVersion is missing or mismatched, the side panel shows a clear warning to the user (e.g., 'MCP server version mismatch — please restart the server')
The warning does not block normal operation — the side panel continues to work best-effort
The PROTOCOL_VERSION is a simple integer that only increments when the config.getState response shape changes in a breaking way
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
This addresses the 'Remaining Concern #2' from the audit: if the managed extension auto-install gets out of sync with the MCP server, the side panel could silently receive unexpected data shapes.

Implementation plan:

1. In platform/shared/src/index.ts, add:
```typescript
/** Protocol version for side panel ↔ MCP server communication.
 *  Increment when the config.getState response shape changes in a breaking way. */
export const SIDE_PANEL_PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1;
```

2. In platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts, handleConfigGetState:
Add `protocolVersion: SIDE_PANEL_PROTOCOL_VERSION` to the result object (import from shared).

3. Update ConfigStateResult in platform/shared/src/index.ts to include `protocolVersion?: number`.

4. In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:
After fetchConfigState() resolves, check if result.protocolVersion matches SIDE_PANEL_PROTOCOL_VERSION. If not, set a warning state. Show a subtle banner below the header when mismatched.

5. The warning should be a small amber/yellow bar with text like 'Server version mismatch — restart the MCP server for best results'. Non-blocking.

The protocolVersion field is optional in ConfigStateResult so that older servers without it gracefully degrade (the side panel treats missing as 'potentially mismatched' but only shows a warning, never blocks).
